creator: scheidell@FreeBSD.org
text:
On 8/8/12 11:40 AM, green dog wrote:
>
> gcc or clang?
Thank you for taking the time to upgrade this library.  Your efforts are 
appreciated.
clang for 9.x, gcc for < 9.x

Just a note: before a ports committer can send a new port, and update, 
or patch to the FreeBSD ports tree, to be downloaded by everyone, we 
must test it against all 'default' configurations, major platforms and 
currently supported OS versions.

This includes running FreeBSD 7.4, 8.2, and 9.0 and AMD64 and I386 
platforms.

If any of these fail, we have to go back to the maintainer or submitter 
and ask them to fix it.

FreeBSD 7.4 uses, by default, gcc.
here is the full log.
<http://lorie.secnap.net/tb/errors/7-scheidell_I386/libobjc2-1.6.1.log>

I think 9.0 uses, by default, gcc, but in this Makefile, it is forced to 
be clang with 9.x.
(which compiles)
<http://lorie.secnap.net/tb/index.php?action=display_markup_log&build=9-scheidell_AMD64&id=2259#144>
full log:
<http://lorie.secnap.net/tb/errors/9-scheidell_AMD64/libobjc2-1.6.1.log>

I suggest that you join the email list, freebsd-ports@, and ask for 
assistance.  And, to do testing, join 'redports', where you can do test 
compiles in various mach/arch combinations.

Once you have all this worked out, reply back to bug-followup, with an 
ascii/text patch attached, and I will run it in my build machine and 
make sure it compiles and packages.

If you think it will take more than two weeks to do this, then you might 
want to close this pr and open a new one.
You might also want to consider becoming the official maintainer of this 
port so that you can make sure it is kept up to date.
-- 
Michael Scheidell, CTO
 >*| * SECNAP Network Security Corporation
d: +1.561.948.2259
w: http://people.freebsd.org/~scheidell
